title: SLSS 2023 25 Strategic Plan V1
body:
STRATEGIC & OPERATIONAL PLAN 2023-2025 Version 1 - August 2023   OUR STRATEGIC PLAN Surf Life Saving Sydney (SLSS) is a coordination and representative body of Surf Life Saving for the fifteen Surf Life Saving Clubs & various support operation stretching from North Bondi south to Burning Palms in the Royal National Park. The role of SLSS is to coordinate lifesaving activities including: club based patrols & support services, surf sports events, youth leadership, junior member growth & development along with support to members. Our team of trainers, assessors & facilitators provide our surf lifesavers with an extensive & integrated award structure: Our Junior Activities (nipper) programs cater for over 6,400 junior members, who range in age from 5 to 13 years & is a stepping stone for many of our surf lifesavers & champion competitors of the future. SLSS is a vibrant volunteer community & emergency service organisation, with over 18,840 volunteer members providing the Sydney community with a world class water safety service. This strategy has been developed in close collaboration & partnership with Clubs, the Board & officers & staff. This document provides guidance on;

OUR VISION OUR PRIORITIES The future we want Our focus to help achieve our vision, purpose & values

PRIORITY PRIORITY 1 2

OUR PURPOSE OUR ACTIVITY Our role in making it happen What we do

GOVERNANCE ADMINISTRATION FINANCE EDUCATION LIFESAVING MEMBER SERVICES SURF SPORTS YOUTH & JUNIOR

OUR VALUES OUR STRUCTURE The behaviours that drive our culture Our officers & committees that drive what we do

SURF LIFE SAVING SYDNEY | STRATEGIC AND OPERATIONAL PLAN 2023-2025

OUR VISION Zero preventable deaths in Australian waters.

OUR FOCUS Help Clubs & Lifesavers be their best while striving to prevent drowning & injury along the Sydney coastline.

OUR PURPOSE We save lives, create great Australians & build better communities.

OUR FOCUS Develop skilled lifesavers & leaders while fostering club & community collaboration & connection.

OUR VALUES Our values are aspirational & guide our directors, officers & members. We know that a good culture is where the behaviours match the rhetoric & we strive to incorporate these values in our day to day operations.

• SAFETY • TRUST • RESPECT • CARING • RESPONSIBILITY

What the SLSA values mean to Surf Life Saving Sydney. SAFETY - FIRST We develop a culture of safety with our members & with the public.

TRUST We develop trust through our actions. Protecting the flags & beaches on the frontline & by being open & honest with each other behind the line.

RESPECT People come to the beach for fun. We respect the public, the ocean & each other. We act with integrity & model respectful behaviour.

CARING We support & exhibit concern for people on & off the beach. We value life & well-being.

RESPONSIBILITY We are compliant, accountable & deliver on what we say we will.

SURF LIFE SAVING SYDNEY | STRATEGIC AND OPERATIONAL PLAN 2023-2025   OUR PRIORITIES The following two priorities are three-year focus areas for each of our directors and officers for 2023-2025.

Ongoing Development Priorities for SLSS Board 2023/24

PRIORITY 1 The following priorities continue to be the focus of the SLSS Board. Making it easy for 1. Communicate with our stakeholders volunteers, staff & the better community to contribute to We’ll develop more targeted the movement. communication processes using coordinated technologies to allow officers, Identifying pain points. members and external stakeholders to Being a thought leader on change. have better access to information relevant Be solution focused & leaders on what to them and to decrease information that needs improving. does not relate to them. Advocating for it to be easier & better. 2. Develop a Branch wide Culture of Safety We’ll focus on our value of ‘Safety First’ by developing activity that enhance safe 6 behaviours across our membership. 3. Shift our leadership to a more strategic focus We’ll enhance our practices by focusing more on better leadership practices. PRIORITY 2 This will include enhancing our business calendar, Board agenda, Branch Council Growing & developing what involvement, action plans, dashboard, gender equity and greater understanding we do best – saving lives of community needs. Knowing our data. 4. Drive our activity and club support Develop growth priorities through professional support & strategies. Bring others along & guild alignment. Protect our volunteers from burnout and Provide greater service in better ways. support projects by increased professional Connecting, collaborating & advocacy support. We’ll aim to reduce the branch internally & externally. reliance on financial support from clubs. 5. Capture our corporate knowledge to provide clarity to future officers Clearly define our ways of working through the capture of ways of working, operations manuals, project plans and action plans.

OUR ACTIVITY DEVELOPMENT The following priorities have been identified for Season 2023/24 as development areas for the Board and Committees.

GOVERNANCE ADMINISTRATION President • Continue developing the operations manual to • Create alignment with values, purpose and plan capture our corporate knowledge and membership – update BOM strategic focus • Embed Office 365 for digital communication and and calendar storage • Implement improved engagement of Public • Refresh By-Laws and Constitution and align with Safety in National Parks Australian Standards • Work closer with SLSNSW / SLSA to ensure our • Co-ordinate Social Media planning and clubs are consulted to their needs engagement Deputy President • Introduce stronger governance for Council, • Continue Ramsgate transition strategy Board, Committee performance through the • Grow and enhance partnership engagement BoardPro software platform

FINANCE EDUCATION • Review investment strategy • Roll out of new Beach Management qualification • Continue to develop a relevant chart of accounts • PUA20119 transition for BM TAFs and TAFs • Identify needs to club Finance Director requiring HLTAID001

LIFESAVING MEMBER SERVICES Lifesaving • Drive culture of safety roll out across 11 • Facilitate required funding an equipment functional areas available for call out teams responding to • Develop advisory groups with a focus on Floods/ Fire actions women, inclusive and member engagement • Better Member engagement through: • Grow club member service focus - Developing Leadership roles / pathways and engagement within Branch • Roll-out healthy club checklist - Opening Branch LSOCX operations to the • Conduct a round of Quick Chats for greater membership and encouraging participation member engagement an increase quality of - Patrol Captain Interclub patrolling services witin clubs - Engaging the membership from a Branch • Deliver 2 x Club Development Days - F2F Space not just a club • Promote Member Recognition and AoE Support Operations • Develop Support Operations Hub at Malabar • Station all EB RWCs at Malabar • Station RWC in Botany Bay with particular emphasis on Yarra Bay to Cruwee Cove • Further roll out of eBikes • Full Integration of SR30 with Support Operations • Continue working toward 30 Duty Officers • Maintain high level of service during Summer Peak Period

SURF SPORTS YOUTH & JUNIOR • Deliver a great athlete and parent experience at • Undertake a number of specialist junior clinics to the Branch Championships support membership in the areas of pool • Run a successful Branch Championships rescue, • Continue to utilise Live heats for all carnivals in water safety, sports development, and coaching the Sydney Branch • Expand existing camp development program • Identify development opportunities for all • Implement coaching accreditation sections of surf sports in 23/24 season to grow • Explore connection with Dragon participation in specified section and/or Boat competition age groups.
